item.title: EasyJet rejects £4.7bn takeover bid from Castlelake
item.description: US private credit group goes public with latest 625p-a-share offer after airline’s board refuses to engage

item.title: Robots will replace 700,000 delivery workers ‘sooner or later’, warns JD.com boss
item.description: China’s rapid adoption of technology threatens millions of gig-economy jobs, policymakers fear

item.title: China restricts trading with some US rare earth companies
item.description: Beijing introduces more export controls in retaliation for Washington’s ‘wrongful’ actions

item.title: Do US insurers arbitrage capital regulations with private ratings?
item.description: Probably!

item.title: FirstFT: Starmer’s exit plans expected
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Middle East conflict and Bain’s vibecoding

item.title: Italy open to Germany joining fighter jet project, says Leonardo chief
item.description: Lorenzo Mariani says Berlin role in GCAP would slow progress but bring benefits after collapse of rival FCAS programme

item.title: UK’s out-of-town retail parks ‘effectively full’
item.description: Industry says shortage of space reflects rising demand from some retailers and a dearth of new sites

item.title: Iran war supercharges electric vehicle uptake in Africa
item.description: Boom in demand for electric motorbikes and buses on continent is a boost for China

item.title: New home builds set to fall short of government’s 1.5mn target, Savills says
item.description: Real estate group’s forecast adds to questions over housebuilding agenda in England

item.title: What would a stablecoin run look like?
item.description: Fire sales of assets held in reserve could trigger problems in many other financial markets

item.title: Luxury’s race to secure Milan’s hidden palazzos
item.description: In a bid for the next level of exclusivity, brands pay tens of thousands of euros a day to stage events in the city’s historic residences

item.title: How tennis agents spot the next stars
item.description: Identifying talent rarely follows an exact formula, but building up a network of contacts still acts as the best guide

item.title: Wimbledon champion Iga Świątek: the walk to Centre Court used to ‘really stress me’
item.description: The no 3 seed at this year’s tournament reflects on how she conquered prematch nerves to win the 2025 women’s singles final

item.title: US Open venue overhaul drives shift to more premium sales
item.description: As players demand ever bigger prize pots, Grand Slam organisers are investing in infrastructure to maximise revenues

item.title: Players call for stronger response to growing problem of extreme heat
item.description: Measures to protect contenders when temperatures surge at tournaments are not always triggered

item.title: Wimbledon and its sponsors draw on power of influencers
item.description: Invitations to the royal box are extended to a mix of talent and celebrity, from Mary Berry to Olivia Rodrigo, but increasingly with Gen Z in mind

item.title: Bain tests software takeover targets by vibecoding AI replicas
item.description: Private equity groups swiftly recreate software products to gauge their competitive advantages

item.title: ATP chair warns tennis divide leads to billions in lost revenue
item.description: Andrea Gaudenzi says professional sport’s $3.5bn annual turnover could double if tours and tournaments combine forces

item.title: ‘We’re here to win’: Nike CEO on the race to revive the biggest brand in sport
item.description: Elliott Hill says scale of problems means restructuring is taking longer than hoped

item.title: FT Financial Literacy and Inclusion Campaign
item.description: The FT invites readers to join our campaign to promote financial literacy in the UK and around the world

item.title: India plays ‘whack-a-mole’ with online financial influencers
item.description: Regulator targets scammers who give stock tips under guise of financial education as ranks of retail investors grow

item.title: Building materials group CRH nears its biggest-ever deal to buy Arcosa
item.description: Irish company closes in on purchase of Dallas-based construction firm with market capitalisation of almost $7bn

item.title: Wars trigger $12bn venture capital rush into defence tech
item.description: Funding this year has already surpassed 2025 total as soaring valuations fuel fears of a hype cycle

item.title: Michael O’Leary in line for €150mn payout in latest Ryanair contract
item.description: Longtime boss of Europe’s biggest airline signs new deal that includes bumper share award if he hits ambitious targets

item.title: Electricity ‘reselling’ adds £99mn to consumer bills
item.description: Regulator blames market design as hydropower stations and batteries repeatedly sell and curtail their output

item.title: Michael Bloomberg arms green lobby for fight against oil interests
item.description: Billionaire pledges $285mn to renewable industry associations as nations face energy policy decisions in wake of Iran war

item.title: UK telecoms groups introduce ‘kill switch’ to cut phone theft from stores
item.description: Virgin Media O2 and VodafoneThree’s move comes after Apple and Samsung resisted calls to adopt broader measures

item.title: SpaceX handed lowest possible ESG rating by MSCI
item.description: Triple C score puts Elon Musk’s company on par with Russia after 2022 invasion of Ukraine

item.title: Workers are emerging as the next big AI logjam
item.description: Big Tech wakes up to the need for brawn and crafts skills to build and maintain data centres

item.title: Holiday bookings surge in markets hit by Iran crisis
item.description: Rebound across eastern Mediterranean as travellers reassess conflict’s threat and proximity
